name: 374Water Inc.
business: 374Water Inc. is a cleantech and environmental services company developing supercritical water oxidation technology (SCWO) for the destruction of organic waste streams within the municipal, federal, and industrial markets. The company offers its proprietary AirSCWO technology to destroy and mineralize a broad spectrum of non-hazardous and hazardous organic wastes, producing safe dischargeable water, minerals, vent gas, and recoverable heat energy. Products and services include AirSCWO systems, pre- and post-treatment solutions, Waste Destruction Services (WDS), laboratory and testing services, and operations and maintenance services.
sellsTo: Industrial sectors (oil & gas, chemicals, pharmaceuticals, battery recycling)
namedCustomers: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

exposures.sentence: Throughout the year we established our Waste Destruction Services (WDS) hub at the City of Orlando's Iron Bridge Water Reclamation Facility, deployed a commercial AirSCWO system to Detroit, MI, for a six week Department of Defense (DoD) Destruction Demonstration of six PFAS-impacted waste streams, deployed our mobile AirSCWO lab to Peterson Space Force Base in Colorado, and tested waste streams from several clients, including major oil and gas companies, multi-national chemical and pharmaceutical companies, the United States DoD and defense contractors, and resource recovery companies, at our laboratory in the Research Triangle, North Carolina.
The municipal water and wastewater treatment market was estimated to be more than $347 billion annually in 2024, growing at a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of approximately 7.5%.

Throughout the year we established our Waste Destruction Services (WDS) hub at the City of Orlando's Iron Bridge Water Reclamation Facility, deployed a commercial AirSCWO system to Detroit, MI, for a six week Department of Defense (DoD) Destruction Demonstration of six PFAS-impacted waste streams, deployed our mobile AirSCWO lab to Peterson Space Force Base in Colorado, and tested waste streams from several clients, including major oil and gas companies, multi-national chemical and pharmaceutical companies, the United States DoD and defense contractors, and resource recovery companies, at our laboratory in the Research Triangle, North Carolina.

Throughout the year we established our Waste Destruction Services (WDS) hub at the City of Orlando's Iron Bridge Water Reclamation Facility, deployed a commercial AirSCWO system to Detroit, MI, for a six week Department of Defense (DoD) Destruction Demonstration of six PFAS-impacted waste streams, deployed our mobile AirSCWO lab to Peterson Space Force Base in Colorado, and tested waste streams from several clients, including major oil and gas companies, multi-national chemical and pharmaceutical companies, the United States DoD and defense contractors, and resource recovery companies, at our laboratory in the Research Triangle, North Carolina.

Our AirSCWO technology is designed to effectively convert solid and liquid wastes such as sewage sludge, biosolids, food waste, hazardous and non-hazardous waste, including forever chemicals (e.g., per-and polyfluoroalkyl substances or PFAS) into inert and recoverable resources including water, minerals, and heat energy.

catalysts.event: Design and scale AirSCWO systems to handle larger capacities
catalysts.sentence: During 2026, we expect to design and scale our AirSCWO systems to handle larger capacities of slurries and liquid wastes.

catalysts.event: Build out Waste Destruction Services hub at City of Orlando's Iron Bridge Water Reclamation Facility
catalysts.sentence: During 2026, we expect to design and scale our AirSCWO systems to handle larger capacities of slurries and liquid wastes; build out our Waste Destruction Services hub at the City of Orlando's Iron Bridge Water Reclamation Facility; begin to receive and destroy significant volumes of PFAS wastes to our WDS hub.

catalysts.event: Deploy team and technology to Orange County Sanitation District (OC San)
catalysts.sentence: During 2026, we expect to ... deploy our team and technology to Orange County Sanitation District (OC San) in Fountain Valley, CA.

catalysts.event: Deploy mobile AirSCWO system to St. Cloud, MN
catalysts.sentence: During 2026, we expect to ... deploy our mobile AirSCWO system to St. Cloud, MN, to demonstrate its effectiveness destroying PFAS-laden biosolids and other PFAS wastes.

catalysts.event: Negotiate additional TSDF partnerships to expand WDS hubs
catalysts.sentence: During 2026, we expect to ... negotiate additional Treatment, Storage, and Disposal Facility (TSDF) partnerships to expand our WDS hubs.
In March 2026, we issued three separate convertible notes and received cash proceeds of $800,000.

tamClaims.claim: The FAA Reauthorization Act of 2024 included $350 million for the PFAS Replacement Program for Airports.
tamClaims.sentence: To facilitate this process the FAA Reauthorization Act of 2024 included $350 million for the PFAS Replacement Program for Airports to support the transition to F3.
